fixture.prompt: I do not know which visual direction is right. Show several concrete options I can react to.

baseline_invocation.input: I do not know which visual direction is right. Show several concrete options I can react to.

candidate_invocation.input: I do not know which visual direction is right. Show several concrete options I can react to.

mechanical_port.allowed_difference: Canonical GStack 2 carve: exclude the retired shared onboarding wrapper and host hook advisory; resolve retired invocations to six public routes; relocate host/runtime paths; lazy-load pinned carved sections from package-local references.

differences.explanation:
### User-language rule

Write questions, progress updates, reports, and artifacts in the language used by the user. Source material, code identifiers, commands, and quotations may remain in their original language when translating them would reduce accuracy.

differences.reproduced_defect: Retain rejection confidence in design exploration
differences.explanation:
### Rejection-strength memory

When recording design feedback, preserve how explicit and confident a rejection was. A hard rejection becomes a strong negative constraint; tentative dislike remains a weak signal that can be revisited. Never flatten rejected directions into evidence equivalent to approved directions.
